title: Backfill usage events missing cache-hit attribution fields (Hosted API)
preview:
## Context
We have a measurable spike in NULL/unknown values for cache attribution fields on `usage_events` for Hosted API traffic. This breaks:
- Billing attribution for cache hits vs non-cache hits (needed for correct unit economics and some packaging rules)
- Experiment analysis where variants change caching beha...
text:
## Context
We have a measurable spike in NULL/unknown values for cache attribution fields on `usage_events` for Hosted API traffic. This breaks:
- Billing attribution for cache hits vs non-cache hits (needed for correct unit economics and some packaging rules)
- Experiment analysis where variants change caching behavior (needs clean segmentation)
- Finance reconciliation queries (NULL dimensions inflate “unknown bucket” and make drift checks noisier)

This ticket covers a *repair/backfill* for usage events already written with missing cache fields, plus hardening to prevent recurrence.

## What’s missing
Fields observed as NULL or defaulted incorrectly on a subset of events:
- `cache_hit` (boolean)
- `cache_hit_type` (e.g., prefix_cache / kv_cache / response_cache) — if present in schema; otherwise leave blank and only set `cache_hit`
- `cache_read_tokens` / `cache_saved_tokens` (if a...
tags: Billing meter hardening + pricing experiment integrity (Q1 2026)

title: Build metering sanity dashboard in Console (freshness, drift vs ledger, duplicates/nulls)
preview:
Context
Finance/RevOps need a first-class Console dashboard to validate billing meter integrity daily/weekly. Today we have scattered SQL snippets + Grafana panels, but no unified view with drilldowns + annotations + ownership. This dashboard is a core deliverable in the metering hardening initiative: small metering...
text:
Context
Finance/RevOps need a first-class Console dashboard to validate billing meter integrity daily/weekly. Today we have scattered SQL snippets + Grafana panels, but no unified view with drilldowns + annotations + ownership. This dashboard is a core deliverable in the metering hardening initiative: small metering bugs (double-counting retries, missing dimensions, late events) are causing invoice disputes and noisy pricing experiment conclusions.

Goal
Ship a Console dashboard (internal-only, Finance/RevOps accessible) that surfaces:
1) Data freshness for usage-event and billing-ledger tables
2) Drift between usage-event aggregates and billing ledger (by account/day/model/region)
3) Duplicate/idempotency signals + null/missing-dimension coverage
4) Quick links to runbooks, canonical queries, and recent known incidents

Non-goals (for this ticket)
- Implementing metering logic change...
tags: Billing meters hardening + pricing experiment integrity (Finance/Legal/RevOps)

title: Alert when usage-event aggregates drift from billing ledger (by account/model/region)
preview:
## Context
Finance/RevRec is seeing invoice disputes and noisy pricing experiment readouts when telemetry usage events don’t reconcile to the billing ledger. We need automated alerting when aggregated usage events drift vs the billing ledger beyond agreed thresholds, segmented by account/model/region (and endpoint w...
text:
## Context
Finance/RevRec is seeing invoice disputes and noisy pricing experiment readouts when telemetry usage events don’t reconcile to the billing ledger. We need automated alerting when aggregated usage events drift vs the billing ledger beyond agreed thresholds, segmented by account/model/region (and endpoint where applicable).

This ticket is the alerting + SLO layer that sits on top of:
- the ledger reconciliation export/table (Finance-facing)
- the usage-event aggregate tables (telemetry)

Primary goal: catch billing-impacting drift early (daily) and make it actionable with clear owners, drill-down queries, and runbook steps.

## Definitions
- **Usage events**: request-level metering events produced by the Hosted API + Dedicated meters after dedupe/idempotency.
- **Billing ledger**: the invoicing source-of-truth for billed units (tokens/requests/embeddings units; Dedicated GPU...
tags: Billing meter hardening + pricing experiment integrity (Finance/Legal/RevOps)

title: Implement Dedicated GPU-seconds meter v2 (idle + autoscaling transition definitions)
preview:
## Context
We’ve had repeated confusion (and at least one customer escalation) around Dedicated usage reporting because our current GPU-hours/GPU-seconds logic does not have crisp, implementable rules for:
- idle time vs “available capacity” vs “in-use capacity”
- autoscaling transitions (pool scale-up/scale-down, i...
text:
## Context
We’ve had repeated confusion (and at least one customer escalation) around Dedicated usage reporting because our current GPU-hours/GPU-seconds logic does not have crisp, implementable rules for:
- idle time vs “available capacity” vs “in-use capacity”
- autoscaling transitions (pool scale-up/scale-down, instance drains)
- partial-hour rounding differences between exports vs invoice line items

This ticket implements **GPU-seconds Meter v2** for Dedicated so Finance/RevRec can reconcile usage -> ledger consistently and so Customer Success can explain usage to customers without “it depends on autoscaling” handwaving.

This is part of the broader metering hardening initiative (shadow billing, drift checks, and month-end close operationalization).

## Goal
Ship a v2 metering pipeline for Dedicated that:
1) matches the **formal meter definition** (see Confluence link)
2) provide...

title: Fix token double-counting when server-side retry/fallback occurs (esp. streaming)
preview:
Problem
- We are double-counting tokens on some server-side retry/fallback paths, most visible for streaming responses. A single customer request can produce >1 usage_event rows (or >1 metered aggregation) when:
 1) upstream model backend times out mid-stream and we retry on same model pool, or
 2) smart router fall...
text:
Problem
- We are double-counting tokens on some server-side retry/fallback paths, most visible for streaming responses. A single customer request can produce >1 usage_event rows (or >1 metered aggregation) when:
 1) upstream model backend times out mid-stream and we retry on same model pool, or
 2) smart router falls back to a compatible model variant/region, or
 3) gateway retries after a partial write / transient billing hook error.
- Result: billed_tokens (and sometimes request_count) exceed what customer sees in the Usage API export or what the model actually produced. This is already causing dispute tickets and undermines current pricing experiments.

Scope
- Hosted API token metering for chat/completions endpoints (OpenAI-compatible + Redwood native).
- Focus on server-side retry/fallback only. Client-side retries should be attributed to distinct request_ids unless they reuse id...

- Rollout controller needs low-latency access to: burn...
text:
Implement the rollout-scoped SLO query adapter used by the rollout controller’s SLO evaluator. Adapter must call slo-toolkit burn-rate endpoint(s), enforce staleness rules, and add caching to keep evaluation loops from overloading telemetry/query infra.

Context
- Rollout controller needs low-latency access to: burn-rate status (fast/slow windows), sample sizes / request volume, and "data freshness" for the exact rollout scope (route/service, model version, region, customer tier).
- We have learned from simulation/postmortem that missing/stale monitors must be detectable and must not trigger unsafe rollbacks.

Scope / Deliverables
1) Adapter interface + implementation
- Create MonitorAdapter implementation (go package `rollouts/slo/adapter` or equivalent per repo conventions) that can:
 - Resolve monitor identifier(s) for a rollout scope (by labels) OR take explicit monitor IDs from p...

title: Telemetry coverage dashboard for sensitive-action audit events (expected vs observed + alerts)
preview:
Goal
Build an internal coverage dashboard and alerting that verifies we are emitting audit events for 100% of “sensitive actions” across Hosted/Dedicated/Private. Dashboard must show expected event types vs observed event types, broken down by producer service and deployment mode. Add automated alerts for regression...
text:
Goal
Build an internal coverage dashboard and alerting that verifies we are emitting audit events for 100% of “sensitive actions” across Hosted/Dedicated/Private. Dashboard must show expected event types vs observed event types, broken down by producer service and deployment mode. Add automated alerts for regressions (missing or dropping events).

Why
- We need a repeatable, reviewable way to prove coverage (internal security/compliance + customer security reviews).
- We need regression detection: new endpoints/features frequently ship without audit emission.
- We need pipeline health separation from coverage (i.e., differentiate “service never emits” vs “sink is down”).

Scope (this ticket)
1) Coverage model + data source
- “Expected” set comes from the sensitive-actions event taxonomy registry (ENG-4822). Expected list includes:
 - event_type (canonical string)
 - required_dimension...
tags: Audit Logging Standardization (Schema v1 + 100% Sensitive Actions Coverage)

title: Add standard metrics for rollout SLO evaluation outcomes (label/cardinality compliant)
preview:
Problem
We’re wiring rollout controller decisions (pause/rollback/suppress) to SLO monitors. We need canonical metrics to:
- track SLO evaluation outcomes per scope (service/route/region/tier) without exploding cardinality
- power dashboards/alerts (auto-rollback triggered, suppressed, missing/stale monitor)
- suppo...
text:
Problem
We’re wiring rollout controller decisions (pause/rollback/suppress) to SLO monitors. We need canonical metrics to:
- track SLO evaluation outcomes per scope (service/route/region/tier) without exploding cardinality
- power dashboards/alerts (auto-rollback triggered, suppressed, missing/stale monitor)
- support debugging and postmortems without relying solely on logs

Key requirement: keep metric label sets aligned with observability standards and avoid high-cardinality labels (rollout_id, model_version, request_id, customer_id).

Scope
1) Add controller + evaluator metrics:
- evaluation attempts + outcome
- decision outcomes (none/pause/rollback/suppress)
- reason codes (bounded)
- staleness/no-data tracking
- evaluator timing (optional but useful for capacity planning)

2) Labeling rules + enforcement:
- define allowed label keys + allowed values for bounded enums
- remove/av...

title: Usage insights: build route+model aggregation dataset (workspace/project x time bucket)

This ticket covers building the core aggregated table(s), including partitioning, late-event handling, and schema seman...
text:
## Context
We need a stable, performant aggregated dataset to power in-console usage insights: cost/usage breakdown by API route + model (and optionally deployment mode, region, workspace/project). This ticket covers building the core aggregated table(s), including partitioning, late-event handling, and schema semantics.

This dataset is the source-of-truth for interactive queries behind the Usage Insights Query Service (ENG-3814) and must reconcile with billing totals within defined tolerances (ENG-3817).

## Goals / Non-goals
### Goals
- Aggregate metering/telemetry into time-bucketed rollups keyed by:

title: Kernel auto-selection v2: per-request telemetry fields + sampling policy
preview:
## Goal
Add per-request telemetry for kernel auto-selection (v2) with a sampling policy that keeps overhead + metric cardinality bounded, while enabling:
- rollout monitoring (selection distribution + perf deltas)
- fast triage for tail-latency spikes / suspected mis-selections
- deterministic replay/debug (record i...
text:
## Goal
Add per-request telemetry for kernel auto-selection (v2) with a sampling policy that keeps overhead + metric cardinality bounded, while enabling:
- rollout monitoring (selection distribution + perf deltas)
- fast triage for tail-latency spikes / suspected mis-selections
- deterministic replay/debug (record inputs to the decision, not the prompt)

This ticket covers:
1) finalizing the telemetry schema (fields + naming)
2) implementing emission in runtime (events + counters/histograms)
3) sampling policy + controls (defaults, canary overrides, and “escalation” behavior)
4) dashboards/queries compatibility constraints (cardinality limits)

Non-goals:
- storing prompts or token content (PII/security)
- end-to-end dashboards (tracked elsewhere; this ticket provides the data contract)

## Context
Kernel auto-selection v2 makes a decision keyed on model family/architecture, seq-lengt...

title: Emit telemetry events for Optimization Suggestions funnel (UI + API)
preview: We need a closed-loop telemetry funnel for the new Console “Optimization suggestions” beta (batching/caching only). This ticket covers emitting analytics events from the Suggestions UI and adding lightweight server-side logging for Suggestions API usage so we can (a) measure feature adoption, (b) understand where us...
text:
We need a closed-loop telemetry funnel for the new Console “Optimization suggestions” beta (batching/caching only). This ticket covers emitting analytics events from the Suggestions UI and adding lightweight server-side logging for Suggestions API usage so we can (a) measure feature adoption, (b) understand where users drop, and (c) debug “why is this empty / why is this estimate weird” reports without relying on screenshots.

Scope (beta):
- UI events from the Suggestions list view and details drawer (impressions → expand → dismiss → apply click / deep link).
- API-side events for /optimize/suggestions list + get-by-id calls (success/failure + latency bucket + response shape fingerprints, but NO request/response bodies).
- Ensure events are org/project scoped and do not include PII (no prompt text, no user-entered labels).
- Provide a stable event taxonomy and properties that can sup...

title: Top cost drivers service: compute ‘what changed’ deltas + explainability strings
preview:
Build the backend computation for Usage Insights “Top cost drivers” and “What changed?” views.

We need deterministic, non-misleading explanations for cost changes for a given scope (workspace/project, time range, filters), primarily broken down by (route, model bucket), with additional drivers (tokens in/out, cache...
text:
Build the backend computation for Usage Insights “Top cost drivers” and “What changed?” views.

We need deterministic, non-misleading explanations for cost changes for a given scope (workspace/project, time range, filters), primarily broken down by (route, model bucket), with additional drivers (tokens in/out, cache hit rate, retries, fallbacks, streaming overhead, context length distribution, latency-correlated spend).

Deliverables:
- Heuristic definitions + guardrails (thresholds, confidence, suppression rules)
- Driver ranking algorithm (contribution to $ delta)
- Explainability strings (customer-facing copy templates + evidence metrics)
- Experiments/feature flags for tuning thresholds & copy
- Unit tests + fixtures for common scenarios

Primary consumer: Usage Insights Query API (ENG-3814) and Console UI cards (DES-913 / ENG-3815).

Links:
- Brainstorm: sources/google_drive/shar...

title: Add unit/integration tests for streaming token metering (partials + disconnects)
preview:
Background
We’ve had multiple investigations (Finance + Support) where billed tokens appear to be double-counted or inconsistent for streaming responses, especially when:
- the client disconnects mid-stream
- the server retries/fallbacks mid-stream
- partial chunks are emitted and then the request terminates with a ...
text:
Background
We’ve had multiple investigations (Finance + Support) where billed tokens appear to be double-counted or inconsistent for streaming responses, especially when:
- the client disconnects mid-stream
- the server retries/fallbacks mid-stream
- partial chunks are emitted and then the request terminates with a timeout/cancel

This ticket adds regression coverage so metering invariants are enforced in CI/staging. Goal is to prevent a recurrence of the double-counting bugs seen in the retry/fallback path and to make the semantics explicit for RevRec.

Scope
1) Unit tests at the token meter boundary:
- Streamed chunk accounting matches final “billed_tokens” record
- Partial stream + client disconnect does NOT bill completion tokens that were never emitted/committed
- Server-side retry/fallback within a request does NOT double count prompt tokens
- Tool/function-call deltas do not ca...

title: Telemetry input contracts + data quality checks for Standard Cost Model v1
preview:
## Context
We’re standardizing cost computation across Console + Optimize. The Cost Model API v1 needs consistent telemetry inputs (units, semantics, and availability guarantees) so that:
- Console cost breakdowns are stable and comparable over time.
- Optimize what-if projections have the right batch/cache/quant si...
text:
## Context
We’re standardizing cost computation across Console + Optimize. The Cost Model API v1 needs consistent telemetry inputs (units, semantics, and availability guarantees) so that:
- Console cost breakdowns are stable and comparable over time.
- Optimize what-if projections have the right batch/cache/quant signals.
- Finance reporting can reconcile predicted/request-attributed cost vs observed spend with known/controlled drift.

Today we have multiple partially-overlapping sources for token metering and runtime metrics (prefill/decode split, KV cache hits, batch sizes), with naming drift and unclear null/unknown semantics.

This ticket defines **telemetry input contracts** (schemas + semantics + required SLIs) and implements **data quality checks** so Cost Model API can treat inputs deterministically.

## Scope (v1)
### Telemetry inputs to formalize (contracted fields)
1) **Tok...

title: Usage Insights: cardinality guardrails + sampling for route/model breakdowns
preview:
Problem
Usage Insights requires grouping usage/cost by route + model. Without guardrails, the route dimension can explode (e.g., untemplated IDs, user-defined paths, OpenAI-compat proxies), driving:
- warehouse costs (scan + group-by)
- slow/timeout queries for 7–30d ranges
- noisy/misleading “top drivers” (long tai...
text:
Problem
Usage Insights requires grouping usage/cost by route + model. Without guardrails, the route dimension can explode (e.g., untemplated IDs, user-defined paths, OpenAI-compat proxies), driving:
- warehouse costs (scan + group-by)
- slow/timeout queries for 7–30d ranges
- noisy/misleading “top drivers” (long tail dominates)
- ingestion issues (high-cardinality tags)

This ticket implements guardrails across ingestion/aggregation/query to keep cardinality bounded while preserving useful reporting.

Goals
1) Prevent route dimension explosion in the aggregated dataset and query service.
2) Handle untemplated or unknown paths safely (bucket + visibility).
3) Cap query complexity so the console remains interactive (and export remains reliable).
4) Add alerts so we detect regressions early (new route patterns, normalization failures, abusive queries, etc.).

Non-goals
- Changing the can...

title: Instrumentation: add/verify normalized route tag on metering events at ingestion
preview:
Usage Insights requires a stable `route` dimension for cost attribution by API route + model. Today a non-trivial subset of metering events arrive without a reliable route tag (notably streaming endpoints and some OpenAI-compat paths), which leads to “Unknown route” buckets and mismatches vs expectations.

This tick...
text:
Usage Insights requires a stable `route` dimension for cost attribution by API route + model. Today a non-trivial subset of metering events arrive without a reliable route tag (notably streaming endpoints and some OpenAI-compat paths), which leads to “Unknown route” buckets and mismatches vs expectations.

This ticket adds/verified route tags in metering events at ingestion:
- Prefer emitted route fields from gateway when present.
- If missing, derive from request metadata available at ingestion (HTTP method + raw path), then normalize/canonicalize via the shared route normalization library.
- Record provenance (emitted vs derived) to support debugging and rollout.

This is intended to unblock accurate aggregations in ENG-3812 and reduce support issues like missing route breakdowns and unexplained “unknown route” spend.

title: Shadow traffic telemetry events v1 (shadow request/response pair + scoring refs) with cardinality + PII constraints
preview:
Goal
Ship v1 telemetry events and ingestion logic for shadow traffic pairs so we can compute quality deltas (control vs shadow) and attach them to rollouts/experiments without blowing up metrics cardinality or leaking sensitive prompt data.

Background
Shadow traffic duplicates a sampled subset of production request...
text:
Goal
Ship v1 telemetry events and ingestion logic for shadow traffic pairs so we can compute quality deltas (control vs shadow) and attach them to rollouts/experiments without blowing up metrics cardinality or leaking sensitive prompt data.

Background
Shadow traffic duplicates a sampled subset of production requests to one or more “shadow targets” while returning the control response to the end user. We need structured telemetry that:
- correlates control + shadow results via stable fingerprinting and bucketing
- captures minimal request/response metadata required for latency/cost/error and quality scoring
- references (not embeds) redacted artifacts for later scoring
- enforces strict cardinality limits across Hosted/Dedicated/Private

Primary stakeholders
- Telemetry/Analytics: Logan Wright (lead), Nadia Rahman (assignee), Caleb Johnson
- Routing: Ravi Menon / Mina Haddad
- Applied...
tags: Shadow traffic tool for eval metrics

title: Add/adjust dashboard panels for KV cache compaction (fragmentation, bytes moved, evictions, p99 correlation)
preview:
Goal
Add/adjust the standard runtime/SRE dashboards to support safe rollout + ongoing operations of KV cache compaction and tier-aware eviction. This ticket is explicitly about panels and dashboard ergonomics (not changing metric emission).

Why
We need to (a) see fragmentation behavior over time, (b) understand com...
text:
Goal
Add/adjust the standard runtime/SRE dashboards to support safe rollout + ongoing operations of KV cache compaction and tier-aware eviction. This ticket is explicitly about panels and dashboard ergonomics (not changing metric emission).

Why
We need to (a) see fragmentation behavior over time, (b) understand compaction cost (bytes moved/time) and eviction rate, and (c) correlate compaction/eviction activity to tail latency (p95/p99) during mixed-length bursts. During canary, oncall needs a single dashboard that answers: “Are we compacting because we’re fragmented?”, “Are we compacting too aggressively (latency spikes)?”, “Are we evicting at the right time/tier?”, and “Should we rollback/flip kill-switch?”.

Scope
1) New dashboard (or new section on existing Runtime / Allocator Health dashboard): “KV Cache Compaction”.
2) Required panels (minimum viable for canary):
 - Fragmentatio...
links: Confluence: KV cache fragmentation metrics standard (sources/confluence/architecture-and-standards/performance-standards/kv-cache-fragmentation-metrics-standard.json)
Confluence: Hosted API tail latency guardrails (sources/confluence/eng-sre/slo-and-error-budgets/hosted-api-tail-latency-guardrails.json)
Runbook: Runtime KV cache compaction runbook (sources/confluence/eng-serving-runtime/runbooks/runtime-kv-cache-compaction-runbook.json)
Slack: eng-sre metric definitions + rollback signals (sources/slack/eng-sre/1767652300.json)

title: Route normalization library (templating IDs) shared by Gateway + Telemetry
preview:
Problem
- Usage Insights requires cost/usage breakdown by API route. Today we emit inconsistent route strings depending on where the event is produced (gateway vs downstream services) and whether a request hit OpenAI-compat vs Redwood-native endpoints.
- Unnormalized paths with IDs (e.g. /v1/workspaces/ws_abc123/pro...
text:
Problem
- Usage Insights requires cost/usage breakdown by API route. Today we emit inconsistent route strings depending on where the event is produced (gateway vs downstream services) and whether a request hit OpenAI-compat vs Redwood-native endpoints.
- Unnormalized paths with IDs (e.g. /v1/workspaces/ws_abc123/projects/prj_9f.../keys/key_...) explode cardinality and make aggregations expensive/unreliable.

Goal
- Ship a single, shared route normalization implementation used by both:
 1) API Gateway request metering/telemetry emitters
 2) Telemetry ingestion/aggregation jobs (as a fallback / validation layer)
- Normalize routes by templating path params (IDs) and standardizing prefixes so aggregation keys are stable.
- Provide tests + a migration/rollout plan to avoid breaking billing reconciliation and existing dashboards.

Non-goals (Phase 1)
- Not a general-purpose URL parser for ...

title: Status page metrics backfill job (historical component health for incident summaries + trends)
preview:
## Context
We’re improving status page transparency by moving to a more granular component taxonomy (product surface x region x capability) and publishing higher-quality post-incident summaries quickly.

A recurring gap: we can draft incident summaries from the incident timeline, but we don’t have reliable historica...
text:
## Context
We’re improving status page transparency by moving to a more granular component taxonomy (product surface x region x capability) and publishing higher-quality post-incident summaries quickly.

A recurring gap: we can draft incident summaries from the incident timeline, but we don’t have reliable historical component health rollups (error rate + latency) for the impacted components over the incident window and for pre/post comparisons. This is slowing Comms Leads down and causing “hand-wavy” impact statements.

This ticket adds a backfill job that populates historical component health metrics so:
- post-incident summaries can include concrete impact metrics (e.g., peak error rate, p95 latency, duration)
- we can trend component health over time and audit status accuracy

## Goals
1) Backfill component-level health rollups for a defined history window (initial proposal: 90 da...

title: Add runtime golden-signal recording rules (rate, errors, latency, saturation, queues) for Private minimum pack
preview:
Goal
Ship a bounded-cardinality set of Prometheus recording rules that compute the runtime “golden signals” for Redwood Private installs. These rules are the stable building blocks used by the minimum pack dashboards + alerts, so they must be:
- cheap to evaluate
- consistent naming
- backward-compatible where possi...
text:
Goal
Ship a bounded-cardinality set of Prometheus recording rules that compute the runtime “golden signals” for Redwood Private installs. These rules are the stable building blocks used by the minimum pack dashboards + alerts, so they must be:
- cheap to evaluate
- consistent naming
- backward-compatible where possible
- safe for customer Prometheus (bounded label cardinality; no per-request / unbounded labels)

Scope
Recording rules for:
1) Request rate (RPS / req/s) and token throughput (tok/s)
2) Error rate (5xx + runtime-level errors)
3) Latency (p50/p95/p99 via histogram quantiles) for end-to-end and model runtime segments where available
4) Saturation (GPU utilization, memory, CPU throttling, request concurrency / in-flight, KV cache pressure if exposed)
5) Queueing / backpressure (queue depth, queue wait time, dropped/limited requests)

Non-goals
- Defining final alert threshol...

title: Support log export to Splunk HEC for Private installs (documented + tested)
preview:
## Context
Private customers frequently mandate forwarding platform logs to Splunk (Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud) via HTTP Event Collector (HEC). Today we have ad-hoc collector configs in PS engagements, which creates support risk and inconsistent outcomes.

This ticket adds a **supported, documented, and valid...
text:
## Context
Private customers frequently mandate forwarding platform logs to Splunk (Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud) via HTTP Event Collector (HEC). Today we have ad-hoc collector configs in PS engagements, which creates support risk and inconsistent outcomes.

This ticket adds a **supported, documented, and validated** Splunk HEC log export path for Redwood Private (VPC + on-prem, including air-gapped constraints).

Scope aligns with the Private observability minimum pack:
- Control plane + data plane operational logs (NOT audit logs; audit logs are handled separately).
- Default collector is the minimum-pack log collector (Vector-based) shipped via `redwood-helm-charts`.

## Goal
Provide a turnkey Splunk HEC sink configuration that:
1) is configurable via Helm values (no editing manifests by hand),
2) uses Kubernetes Secret(s) for tokens,
3) is safe by default (redaction guidance,...

title: Support log export to Elasticsearch/OpenSearch for Private observability minimum pack
preview:
## Summary
Add a supported, documented, and tested configuration for exporting Redwood Private operational logs (control plane + data plane) to Elasticsearch or OpenSearch.

This is part of the Private Observability Minimum Pack (v1). The goal is to provide a turnkey baseline that works in typical customer environme...
text:
## Summary
Add a supported, documented, and tested configuration for exporting Redwood Private operational logs (control plane + data plane) to Elasticsearch or OpenSearch.

This is part of the Private Observability Minimum Pack (v1). The goal is to provide a turnkey baseline that works in typical customer environments while keeping defaults safe (no PII/secrets) and bounded (no unbounded cardinality and no unbounded buffering).

## Scope
In-scope:
- Vector-based log forwarder sink configuration for:
 - Elasticsearch 8.x (with TLS, basic auth and/or API key)
 - OpenSearch 2.x (compat mode settings)
- Helm values and docs for enabling the sink in Private installs
- Tested examples for common customer permutations:
 - Elastic Cloud-style endpoint with TLS and API key
 - Self-managed Elasticsearch with basic auth and custom CA bundle
 - Self-managed OpenSearch with basic auth
- Day-2 tro...

title: Standardize Private metric names/labels, cap cardinality, and define migrations
preview:
Private installs need a bounded, consistent metric surface that is safe for customer-managed Prometheus (especially on-prem) and stable across upgrades.

This ticket standardizes:
- Metric naming (prefixes, units, suffixes, histogram conventions)
- Label keys and allowed values (to avoid unbounded cardinality)
- Cro...
text:
Private installs need a bounded, consistent metric surface that is safe for customer-managed Prometheus (especially on-prem) and stable across upgrades.

This ticket standardizes:
- Metric naming (prefixes, units, suffixes, histogram conventions)
- Label keys and allowed values (to avoid unbounded cardinality)
- Cross-service consistency (same label keys mean the same thing)
- Backwards-compatibility plan for any renamed/removed metrics (including recording rules and dashboards)

Primary problems observed in early Private staging and customer escalations:
- High-cardinality labels (route/model/tenant/request_id) causing TSDB churn and slow Grafana queries
- Inconsistent label sets across gateway/router/runtime making dashboards brittle
- Metric renames between builds without migration notes, leading to blank panels after upgrades

Scope (v1): control plane services, API gateway, routi...

title: Benchmark results service + cache layer for marketing site (versioned JSON/CSV, low-latency, resilient)
preview:
Context
The public benchmark landing page + interactive table will pull benchmark result artifacts (versioned JSON/CSV) generated by benchmark-lab. During launch we expect large, spiky, mostly-read traffic (including bots) and we need the results payloads to be served reliably with low latency, without leaking any i...
text:
Context
The public benchmark landing page + interactive table will pull benchmark result artifacts (versioned JSON/CSV) generated by benchmark-lab. During launch we expect large, spiky, mostly-read traffic (including bots) and we need the results payloads to be served reliably with low latency, without leaking any internal identifiers or creating a thundering-herd effect on storage.

Problem
Directly fetching artifacts from object storage (or via an un-cached internal endpoint) risks:
- cache-miss storms on launch day and on asset refresh
- uneven latency by region
- accidental exposure of non-public data if an artifact is misconfigured
- operational fragility (no clear rollback/version pinning)

Goal
Ship a lightweight “benchmark results service” with an aggressive caching strategy and explicit versioning controls, so marketing site can reliably load results and render the table/filt...

title: Observability dashboards + alerts for PLG signal router (lag, HubSpot failures, task routing volume)
preview:
We have the signal aggregation + HubSpot routing service in flight for the PLG-to-sales pilot. Before we expand cohort/regions we need production-grade observability: a single dashboard AEs/ops can reference for volume + health, plus actionable alerts for the oncall rotation.

This ticket covers:
- Dashboard(s) for ...
text:
We have the signal aggregation + HubSpot routing service in flight for the PLG-to-sales pilot. Before we expand cohort/regions we need production-grade observability: a single dashboard AEs/ops can reference for volume + health, plus actionable alerts for the oncall rotation.

This ticket covers:
- Dashboard(s) for pipeline health and throughput
- Alerting for event lag, HubSpot API errors/429s, and task creation anomalies
- Oncall handoff notes/runbook snippets (what to check first, how to backfill/retry)

Why now: We’ve already seen HubSpot 429s during early testing and we need to avoid silent drops/missed follow-up tasks during the NA pilot.

title: PLG signal quality: noise reduction + suppression rules (MVP)
preview:
Context
We’re seeing early pilot feedback that routed PLG signals can be noisy (false positives) and/or “spammy” (re-notifying on the same underlying behavior). This ticket defines and implements the first set of suppression + quality guardrails in the scoring/routing pipeline.

Primary goals
1) Reduce false positiv...
text:
Context
We’re seeing early pilot feedback that routed PLG signals can be noisy (false positives) and/or “spammy” (re-notifying on the same underlying behavior). This ticket defines and implements the first set of suppression + quality guardrails in the scoring/routing pipeline.

Primary goals
1) Reduce false positives and duplicate notifications without losing legitimate “sales-worthy” accounts.
2) Provide deterministic and auditable suppression behavior (why a signal did/didn’t route).
3) Ensure internal/test orgs never route into HubSpot.

Non-goals (for this ticket)
- Building a full review queue UI (we’ll use HubSpot views + internal logs for now).
- Fully automated outbound sequencing.

Problem statement
During pilot week 1, AEs flagged at least one high-friction false positive (Quartz Commerce) and multiple “repeat pings” driven by daily aggregation windows re-surfacing the same...

title: Create Private Grafana dashboard: Runtime Overview (golden signals + GPU + batching/KV cache)
preview:
## Summary
Build the primary Grafana dashboard shipped in the Private Observability Minimum Pack to monitor the Redwood serving runtime in customer clusters.

This dashboard should be the first stop for:
- Customer platform teams operating Redwood Private (VPC/on-prem)
- Redwood Support/SRE during escalations

## Go...
text:
## Summary
Build the primary Grafana dashboard shipped in the Private Observability Minimum Pack to monitor the Redwood serving runtime in customer clusters.

This dashboard should be the first stop for:
- Customer platform teams operating Redwood Private (VPC/on-prem)
- Redwood Support/SRE during escalations

## Goals
1) Provide a single “Runtime Overview” dashboard that surfaces the highest-impact failure modes:
- Latency regressions (p50/p95/p99) and tail amplification
- Error rates / non-2xx responses / model execution errors
- Saturation signals: GPU utilization, GPU memory pressure, CPU/mem, queue depth, request concurrency
- Throughput signals: requests/sec, tokens/sec, decode vs prefill time, output tokens per request
- Batching + caching behavior: batch sizes, continuous batching effectiveness, KV cache hit rate/evictions (where available)

2) Work in Private constraints:
- B...

title: Private: baseline log collector config (structured parsing, buffering/backpressure, safe defaults)
preview:
## Context
Private installs (VPC + on-prem/air-gapped) need a supported, repeatable log pipeline so customers and Redwood Support/SRE can debug health/perf issues without bespoke integrations.

This ticket implements the **baseline log collector** configuration shipped with the Private installer/Helm charts. Focus i...
text:
## Context
Private installs (VPC + on-prem/air-gapped) need a supported, repeatable log pipeline so customers and Redwood Support/SRE can debug health/perf issues without bespoke integrations.

This ticket implements the **baseline log collector** configuration shipped with the Private installer/Helm charts. Focus is on:
- parsing Redwood’s **structured JSON logs** reliably
- **bounded buffering** and sensible backpressure behavior when sinks are unavailable
- safe defaults (no secrets/PII leakage, predictable resource use)
- packaging friendliness for offline/on-prem

Scope includes collector config + Helm values + minimal docs for operators. Export-specific “recipes” (Splunk HEC / Elasticsearch) are tracked in ENG-19150/ENG-19151.

## Decision (v1)
Use **Fluent Bit** as the default log collector for Private.
Rationale:
- widely deployed in customer K8s environments (low friction)
- ...

title: Inventory existing metrics/dashboards used in Hosted and identify what can ship in Private minimum observability pack
preview:
Goal: compile an inventory of the metrics + Grafana dashboards currently used in Hosted/Dedicated and determine what we can safely and supportably ship as part of the Private observability minimum pack.

Why: Private installs cannot rely on Redwood’s internal telemetry stack assumptions (Mimir, centralized logs, int...
text:
Goal: compile an inventory of the metrics + Grafana dashboards currently used in Hosted/Dedicated and determine what we can safely and supportably ship as part of the Private observability minimum pack.

Why: Private installs cannot rely on Redwood’s internal telemetry stack assumptions (Mimir, centralized logs, internal label conventions, multi-tenant routing). We need a bounded-cardinality, documented set of signals that works on customer-managed Prometheus/Grafana (and air-gapped packaging), with minimal bespoke setup.

Scope:
- Review existing Hosted/Dedicated Grafana dashboards and panels used by SRE/oncall for inference runtime + control plane.
- Identify required underlying Prometheus metrics (including histograms/summaries) and current label sets.
- Note gaps for Private (missing instrumentation, high-cardinality labels, dependencies on internal data sources).
- Recommend a v1...

title: HubSpot integration: company lookup + signal property upserts + notes/tasks with retry/rate-limit handling
preview:
Context
We’re implementing the “PLG-to-sales signal routing” pipeline. Scoring will output qualified signals (company/org-level) and we need to route them into HubSpot reliably without spamming reps or breaching rate limits.

This ticket covers the HubSpot integration service responsibilities:
- resolve a Redwood or...
text:
Context
We’re implementing the “PLG-to-sales signal routing” pipeline. Scoring will output qualified signals (company/org-level) and we need to route them into HubSpot reliably without spamming reps or breaching rate limits.

This ticket covers the HubSpot integration service responsibilities:
- resolve a Redwood org/account to a HubSpot Company
- upsert Company properties (current signal + rollups)
- append an Activity/Timeline note describing the signal event
- create a follow-up Task with deterministic ownership rules
- implement retries + rate-limit handling + audit logs so we can prove what happened and re-drive safely

Goals / Requirements
1) Company lookup
- Primary key: Redwood org_id (preferred) mapped to a HubSpot custom property (e.g., redwood_org_id).
- Secondary: company domain (for legacy self-serve orgs where org_id isn’t backfilled yet).
- If multiple companies match d...

title: Audit exporter observability: metrics, tracing, structured logs + dashboards/alerts
preview:
Goal
Ship production-grade observability for the audit-log-exporter service powering the SIEM-ready audit export beta:
- Metrics (Prometheus) suitable for SLO computation and alerting
- Traces (OpenTelemetry) for adapter debugging + config sync issues
- Structured logs (JSON) that are actionable and safe (PII-minimi...
text:
Goal
Ship production-grade observability for the audit-log-exporter service powering the SIEM-ready audit export beta:
- Metrics (Prometheus) suitable for SLO computation and alerting
- Traces (OpenTelemetry) for adapter debugging + config sync issues
- Structured logs (JSON) that are actionable and safe (PII-minimized)
- Grafana dashboards + alert rules aligned with SRE SLO doc

Context
Exporter delivers canonical audit events to Splunk HEC, Datadog Logs, and S3 with at-least-once semantics. During beta we need rapid triage on:
- Lag spikes (downstream throttling, backpressure, exporter stuck)
- Delivery failures (auth, throttling, bad requests, regressions)
- DLQ growth and replay needs
- Config sync failures from control plane

Non-goals
- Per-tenant/destination metrics in production Prometheus (cardinality). Per-destination details must come from traces/logs and the internal statu...

preview:

We need end-to-end visibility from signup -> first successful API call (TTFS) -> first production traffic (TTFP), plus the step completion funnel for the in-console checklist. This das...
text:
Build the canonical onboarding KPI dashboard and alerting for the guided onboarding / first production launch checklist initiative.

We need end-to-end visibility from signup -> first successful API call (TTFS) -> first production traffic (TTFP), plus the step completion funnel for the in-console checklist. This dashboard will be the source of truth for rollout go/no-go and ongoing regression monitoring.

Primary users:
- Product/DevEx: Nikhil Sharma, Marissa Cole, Alex Martinez, Chris Osei
- Telemetry/Data Eng: Logan Wright, Nadia Rahman, Mei Lin
- SRE/Observability review: Allison Grant, Aisha Bello
- Support enablement: Dev Patel (to correlate top onboarding ticket spikes)

- Confluence spec: sources/confluence/eng-platform/dashboards-and-alerts/onboarding-kpis-dashboard-spec.json
- Metric definition ticket: PM-751 (TTFS/TTFP exact logic + cohorting)

title: Backfill baseline TTFS/TTFP + onboarding funnel for existing tenants (pre-checklist comparison)
preview:
Goal
Backfill baseline Time-to-First-Success (TTFS) and Time-to-First-Production-Traffic (TTFP) for existing tenants so we can compare pre/post Console checklist rollout and quantify funnel improvements.

Why this exists
The checklist + new eventing will only be present after rollout. We need a comparable baseline f...
text:
Goal
Backfill baseline Time-to-First-Success (TTFS) and Time-to-First-Production-Traffic (TTFP) for existing tenants so we can compare pre/post Console checklist rollout and quantify funnel improvements.

Why this exists
The checklist + new eventing will only be present after rollout. We need a comparable baseline for tenants created before the checklist launch to:
- validate metric definitions (PM-751)
- build a pre/post comparison cohort
- sanity-check telemetry quality (e.g., avoid false improvements because we only measure new tenants)

Scope (MVP)
1) Compute TTFS + TTFP for tenants created prior to checklist event emission.
2) Backfill an approximate onboarding funnel (step completion proxy) using existing control plane + gateway + usage/metering signals.
3) Publish results into analytics datasets that the onboarding KPI dashboard can query.
4) Add data quality checks + freshness...
tags: Guided onboarding + first production launch checklist

title: Backfill experiment metrics after attribution schema change (add {experiment_id, variant_id})
preview:
Context
We added experiment attribution dimensions to request telemetry per ADR-048 (route_id, experiment_id, variant_id, model_id, region). During beta we discovered that the Console experiment results dashboard depends on per-variant rollups; without a backfill, results for historical windows show as empty/partial...
text:
Context
We added experiment attribution dimensions to request telemetry per ADR-048 (route_id, experiment_id, variant_id, model_id, region). During beta we discovered that the Console experiment results dashboard depends on per-variant rollups; without a backfill, results for historical windows show as empty/partial (and comparisons are misleading).

This ticket plans + implements a backfill strategy for experiment metrics after the schema change, including:
- cost estimates
- throttling/guardrails
- correctness checks (attribution integrity)
- operational runbook notes

References
- ADR: sources/confluence/architecture-and-standards/decision-records/adr-048-experiment-metrics-attribution-schema.json
- Related work: ENG-9026 (emit dims), ENG-9027 (aggregate rollups), ENG-9028 (guardrails/auto-stop)
- GitHub: PR-28503 (rollup table changes), PR-28622 (streaming retry attribution fix), ...

title: Privacy review: onboarding checklist events are PII-free + retention/compliance aligned
preview:
Context
We are shipping onboarding/progress tracking via a standardized event schema emitted primarily server-side (gateway/control plane) to drive the Console checklist and TTFS/TTFP KPIs.

This ticket is the privacy/security validation pass to ensure:
- Event payloads are PII-free (and do not accidentally capture ...
text:
Context
We are shipping onboarding/progress tracking via a standardized event schema emitted primarily server-side (gateway/control plane) to drive the Console checklist and TTFS/TTFP KPIs.

This ticket is the privacy/security validation pass to ensure:
- Event payloads are PII-free (and do not accidentally capture customer prompts, outputs, headers, or identifiers like emails).
- Retention + residency are aligned with Redwood’s compliance posture and the existing data residency/retention guidance for onboarding telemetry.
- Guardrails exist in code and schema to prevent future regressions.

In scope
- Review onboarding event schema v1 fields + examples used by gateway/control plane + OpenAI-compat layer.
- Validate current emission points and payload construction do not contain PII.
- Confirm retention policy for raw events vs derived checklist state vs aggregated KPIs.
- Confirm pro...

title: Build experiment metrics aggregations (latency/cost/error) for Console by {route, experiment, variant}
preview:

Ship the aggregation datasets/jobs that power the Console “Results” tab for route-level experiments. Console needs per-variant metrics (latency, cost, errors, throughput) with stable dimensions and defensible data quality checks.

text:
Goal
Ship the aggregation datasets/jobs that power the Console “Results” tab for route-level experiments. Console needs per-variant metrics (latency, cost, errors, throughput) with stable dimensions and defensible data quality checks.

Context
- Router/policy engine emits experiment attribution fields (experiment_id, variant_id) into request context and OTel spans/metrics (ENG-9026).
- Console will call a results API that returns deltas + guardrail status. This ticket builds the underlying rollups so results queries are cheap and consistent.
- Must follow canonical schema in ADR-048 (dimensions: route_id, experiment_id, variant_id, model_id, region, plus customer/account identifiers at the appropriate layer).

Non-goals (this ticket)
- No full statistical significance engine; only compute aggregates + basic deltas. (PM explicitly out of scope for GA.)
- No customer-defined metrics ing...

title: Emit onboarding checklist events from API gateway at key lifecycle points
preview:
## Context
We are building a Console onboarding checklist backed by a progress-tracking service. To keep instrumentation consistent across SDKs and reduce client-side drift, the decision (ADR-2025-02) is to emit the core onboarding events server-side wherever possible.

This ticket implements server-side emission in...
text:
## Context
We are building a Console onboarding checklist backed by a progress-tracking service. To keep instrumentation consistent across SDKs and reduce client-side drift, the decision (ADR-2025-02) is to emit the core onboarding events server-side wherever possible.

This ticket implements server-side emission in the API Gateway for Hosted API traffic. These events will be ingested by the onboarding progress service (ENG-5813) and used for funnel reporting (ENG-5815) + checklist step state.

## Goals
- Emit reliable, privacy-safe onboarding events for the checklist funnel.
- Ensure events are idempotent and tolerant of retries / duplicate requests.
- Keep payload cardinality constrained per schema v1.
- Cover the most common early failures (401/403, 429, streaming disconnect confusion) with enough signals to drive checklist state and Support debugging.

## Non-goals (MVP)
- Full cl...
tags: Guided onboarding + first production launch checklist

title: Telemetry pipeline: stage-aware metrics + traces for Dedicated staged rollouts (cardinality-safe)
preview:
Goal
Emit stage-tagged metrics and traces so gate evaluation + dashboards can attribute SLO signals to a *specific rollout stage* for Dedicated pools, without blowing up cardinality.

Context
Dedicated staged rollouts introduce traffic/replica partitioning by stage (e.g., 1%/10%/50%/100%). SRE/Operators need to answ...
text:
Goal
Emit stage-tagged metrics and traces so gate evaluation + dashboards can attribute SLO signals to a *specific rollout stage* for Dedicated pools, without blowing up cardinality.

Context
Dedicated staged rollouts introduce traffic/replica partitioning by stage (e.g., 1%/10%/50%/100%). SRE/Operators need to answer:
- Are errors/latency/saturation worse in the candidate stage partition?
- Did promotion change anything (and when)?
- Are we missing telemetry and should gates be held?

This ticket covers:
- Adding rollout/stage context to request metrics + traces across routing -> data plane.
- Cardinality guardrails (tag allowlist, per-metric tag policy, sampling).
- Making data consumable by stage dashboards + gate queries.

Non-goals
- Customer-defined arbitrary metrics for gating (v1 uses Redwood-defined queries).
- Full experiment analytics (conversion, etc.).

Proposed tagging m...
tags: Dedicated staged rollouts for reserved pools

title: Cache recommendations batch job MVP (per-route hit-rate recommendations)
preview:
Goal
Build the MVP batch job that aggregates prefix/KV cache telemetry into per-route recommendations (internal-first; Console stub can read from the output table). This ticket covers: aggregation logic, output schema, backtesting approach, and basic monitoring/guardrails.

Why
We can ship new cache telemetry, but i...
text:
Goal
Build the MVP batch job that aggregates prefix/KV cache telemetry into per-route recommendations (internal-first; Console stub can read from the output table). This ticket covers: aggregation logic, output schema, backtesting approach, and basic monitoring/guardrails.

Why
We can ship new cache telemetry, but it’s not yet actionable for operators/customers. We need a minimal pipeline that turns: hit-rate + miss reasons + eviction/pressure + wasted prefill into concrete levers (cache knobs, routing locality, canonicalization, rollout/version pinning).

Scope (MVP)
- Nightly batch job (24h lookback; optionally 7d for context) producing a small set of recommendations per {deployment_mode, region, route_id, model_version_id}.
- Input: hourly aggregates derived from the new runtime metrics + gateway tags (no raw prompts; only buckets).
- Output: analytics table (partitioned by day) wi...

title: Emit route experiment attribution dimensions in OTel traces + metrics (incl. streaming + retries)
preview:

We need consistent experiment attribution across traces and key metrics so Console can slice latency/cost/error by {route_id, experiment_id, variant_id} with low overhead and without blowing up cardinality.

- Emitting experiment attribution fields in OTel spans (request + model execut...
text:
## Summary
We need consistent experiment attribution across traces and key metrics so Console can slice latency/cost/error by {route_id, experiment_id, variant_id} with low overhead and without blowing up cardinality.

This ticket covers:
- Emitting experiment attribution fields in OTel spans (request + model execution spans)
- Emitting experiment dimensions on a minimal set of metrics (latency, errors, throughput) and ensuring cost attribution pipeline can join on the same identifiers
- Correctness for streaming, retries, and internal fallbacks
- Guardrails against high-cardinality tags / accidental per-request explosion

## Motivation / Why now
Route-level experiments are gated on trustworthy per-variant metrics. During Phase 1 integration testing we observed attribution drop in staging (esp. streaming + retry paths) and early concerns about cardinality/overhead when tagging OTel + ...

1) Event + tag definitions (what we capture, where it’s emitted, cardi...
text:
Goal
Define a minimal, privacy-safe telemetry spec for Quickstart Templates (RAG + agents) so we can measure adoption + activation ("time-to-first-success"), and so Console/Analytics can segment template-originated traffic.

This ticket produces:
1) Event + tag definitions (what we capture, where it’s emitted, cardinality rules)

4) Dashboard requirements for Console/Analytics

Context
DevEx is shipping language templates + a small CLI (init/doctor) and wants to measure:
- template adoption (created projects, active installs)
- activation (successful first run)
- retention (active installs after N days)
- support deflection (docs/troubleshooting effectiveness)

Constraints
- Must not leak prompts, user content, PII, or customer identifiers.

title: Telemetry: tag Hosted requests with rollout_id + cohort and expose baseline vs canary metrics (cardinality-safe)
preview:
Problem
- Hosted canary rollouts require cohort-level observability so customers + SRE can compare baseline vs canary and auto-stop/rollback on SLO regressions.
- Today we can attribute metrics by route/model/region, but not by rollout cohort. We need cohort tagging end-to-end (data plane -> telemetry ingest -> aggr...
text:
Problem
- Hosted canary rollouts require cohort-level observability so customers + SRE can compare baseline vs canary and auto-stop/rollback on SLO regressions.
- Today we can attribute metrics by route/model/region, but not by rollout cohort. We need cohort tagging end-to-end (data plane -> telemetry ingest -> aggregates -> dashboards/alerts) without causing a metric label cardinality explosion.

Scope
1) Request tagging
- Add request-scoped attributes for active rollout assignment:
 - rollout_id (string UUID)
 - rollout_cohort (enum: baseline|canary|none)
 - rollout_route_id (internal route key; should already exist as route_id)
- Ensure attributes are set consistently for:
 - streaming + non-streaming
 - retries (client retries + internal retries)
 - multi-region routing (cohort should be computed once per request and stay sticky)

2) Metrics emitted (cohort-aware)

title: TTL enforcement + purge job for request/response logs store
preview:
Goal
Implement TTL enforcement (scheduled purge) for the request/response logs data store, driven by the retention policy evaluation result for each tenant. Must be idempotent, observable, and safe under peak load.

Context
This is part of the Enterprise Security & Compliance Foundation (“Retention controls + compli...
text:
Goal
Implement TTL enforcement (scheduled purge) for the request/response logs data store, driven by the retention policy evaluation result for each tenant. Must be idempotent, observable, and safe under peak load.

Context
This is part of the Enterprise Security & Compliance Foundation (“Retention controls + compliance reporting pack”). Request/response logs are one of the primary data classes called out in security reviews; we need predictable deletion semantics and evidence (metrics + exports) that retention is being applied.

In-scope
- Add TTL/purge enforcement for request/response logs store ("request_logs" service DB/table/collection).
- Wire purge behavior to evaluated retention policy (per tenant, per region where applicable).
- Support safe backfills (existing rows) and ongoing incremental purging.
- Emit verification metrics and hook into standard dashboards/alerts.

Non-go...

title: Add metrics + dashboards for residency blocked requests (per-region + per-policy breakdowns)
preview:
Context
- As part of Hosted data residency enforcement, we need auditable + actionable telemetry around policy blocks.
- We need to answer: “Who is being blocked?”, “In which region?”, “By which policy scope (org/project/key)?”, “Is this a misconfig vs a routing fallback attempt?”, and “Is enforcement adding latency...
text:
Context
- As part of Hosted data residency enforcement, we need auditable + actionable telemetry around policy blocks.
- We need to answer: “Who is being blocked?”, “In which region?”, “By which policy scope (org/project/key)?”, “Is this a misconfig vs a routing fallback attempt?”, and “Is enforcement adding latency?”.

Non-goals

Proposed telemetry (authoritative names; align with Observability naming conventions)

- residency.enforce...

title: Audit exporter: Datadog Logs destination adapter (API key, tag mapping, rate-limit/backoff)
preview:
## Context
We need a Datadog Logs destination for the SIEM-ready audit export beta (initial sinks: Splunk HEC, Datadog Logs, generic S3). This ticket covers the Datadog adapter implementation in `audit-log-exporter` including:
- API key handling + secure config plumbing
- Mapping audit event fields into Datadog-frie...
text:
## Context
We need a Datadog Logs destination for the SIEM-ready audit export beta (initial sinks: Splunk HEC, Datadog Logs, generic S3). This ticket covers the Datadog adapter implementation in `audit-log-exporter` including:
- API key handling + secure config plumbing
- Mapping audit event fields into Datadog-friendly tags/facets
- Rate limit detection and backoff strategy (avoid noisy retries / runaway lag)

Datadog should be usable by Security Ops teams who already centralize logs there, and should preserve audit semantics (stable `event_id`, categories, actor/resource metadata) while minimizing PII.

References:
- Confluence: `audit-event-schema-v2-and-siem-mapping.json`
- ADR: `adr-audit-export-delivery-semantics-and-idempotency.json` (at-least-once delivery)
- ADR: `adr-audit-export-filtering-model.json`
- PII policy: `pii-redaction-and-sensitive-fields-policy-for-audit-events....
tags: Enterprise Security & Compliance Foundation — SIEM-ready audit export (Beta)

title: Emit audit log events for residency policy create/update/delete + admin overrides
preview:
## Context
Hosted data residency enforcement introduces a new residency policy object (org/project/API key scope) and optional admin override mechanisms for incident mitigation / support workflows. Enterprise customers and auditors expect a complete, immutable audit trail for:
- policy lifecycle changes (create/upda...
text:
## Context
Hosted data residency enforcement introduces a new residency policy object (org/project/API key scope) and optional admin override mechanisms for incident mitigation / support workflows. Enterprise customers and auditors expect a complete, immutable audit trail for:
- policy lifecycle changes (create/update/delete)
- changes to policy enforcement status (enabled/disabled)
- admin overrides that temporarily relax residency enforcement or allow cross-region fallback

This ticket adds control-plane emitted audit log events and wires them into the audit-log-exporter pipeline.

Links:
- PRD: Hosted data residency enforcement (Confluence)
- Design/Model: Residency policy model + admin UX (Confluence)
- ADR-021: Region pinning enforcement boundaries (Confluence)
- Related work: ENG-4821 (policy schema + validation), ENG-4827 (routing enforcement)
- Implementation PR (target): sour...
tags: Enterprise Security & Compliance Foundation: Hosted data residency enforcement

title: Reconciliation report: unit economics aggregates vs billing totals
preview:
Build a reconciliation view/report that compares the dashboard’s blended $/1M output tokens numerator/denominator components against Billing/RevOps totals to detect discrepancies early.

Why:
- Finance signoff requires we can explain (and quantify) gaps between platform metering aggregates and billed usage/spend inp...
text:
Build a reconciliation view/report that compares the dashboard’s blended $/1M output tokens numerator/denominator components against Billing/RevOps totals to detect discrepancies early.

Why:
- Finance signoff requires we can explain (and quantify) gaps between platform metering aggregates and billed usage/spend inputs.
- SRE/oncall needs a fast way to determine whether a spike/drop is real vs a data pipeline issue.
- Product needs guardrails so exec readouts don’t get derailed by “these numbers don’t match billing”.

Scope (v1):
- Warehouse views that compute comparable aggregates over a shared time window:
 1) Metered tokens (output tokens as primary; include input tokens for debugging)
 2) Metered request counts (streaming vs non-streaming)
 3) Allocated cost numerator used by blended cost (GPU + overhead/idle allocations per ADR)
 4) Billing totals (token-based charges / invoice u...

title: Unit Economics: data quality checks + anomaly alerts (completeness, drift, token drops)
preview:

text:
Goal
Ship the v1 data quality guardrails for the Unit Economics pipeline so Finance/Product/Eng can trust blended $/1M output tokens. Specifically:
- Completeness checks for hourly aggregates and key dimensions (tier/region/model/quantization_profile)
- Drift/anomaly checks (sudden output token drops, spikes in unknown dimension values)
- Alerts routed to the right channels with runbook links and clear severity

Context
We have known fragility around:
- Streaming path output token under-counting (see ENG-8421)
- Region normalization null/unknown mapping (ENG-8424)
- Tier derivation edge cases (ENG-8423)

This ticket adds the monitoring layer so we catch regressions quickly and prevent exec-facing reporting from silently degrading.

Scope (v1)
1) Hourly completeness checks
- Pipeline freshness: hourly partition exists for the last N hours (N=2 for warning, N=4 for critical)
- Hourly co...

title: Warehouse view performance optimization for Unit Economics drilldowns

Context
The internal Unit Economics dashboard (Console) relies on hourly aggregates and drilldown queries (top-N by model/region/tier; time series w/ group_by). text:
Context
The internal Unit Economics dashboard (Console) relies on hourly aggregates and drilldown queries (top-N by model/region/tier; time series w/ group_by). In dogfood, several drilldowns are timing out or returning in 8–25s for common filters (7d / all regions / hosted + dedicated), which is too slow for the intended workflow (Finance + Eng diagnosing regressions).

Problem statement
Warehouse views are currently computed via on-the-fly GROUP BY over the hourly fact table for most endpoints. The fact table is large and growing quickly; common queries scan too many partitions and spill during sort/aggregation. Additionally, some group_bys (model_version + quant_profile) are forcing wide aggregations that defeat caching.

Goal / success criteria
- P95 dashboard query latency in the backend <= 2.0s for:
 - 24h and 7d time series grouped by {model, region, tier}
 - top-N (N<=50) brea...
